design_principles.agent_configs: Dimension config files (d1-d5) are general-purpose. They encode regulatory frameworks, evidence logic, and rubric thresholds. They contain NO company-specific content — no named entities, no example companies, no ground truth labels. They are loaded by the Analysis Agent and must work on any 10-K.
design_principles.ground_truth: ground_truth.json is used ONLY by the evaluation harness. It must never be passed to the Analysis Agent as context. It contains the 15 labeled examples, key evidence descriptions, and evaluation notes.
design_principles.separation_rationale: Mixing ground truth into agent configs would bias the agent toward the test companies and produce inflated accuracy scores that do not generalize.

agent_facing_files.name: Financial Health
agent_facing_files.sp_framework: Financial Risk Profile — cash flow/leverage analysis
agent_facing_files.primary_source: S&P Corporate Methodology Jan 2024 + Altman Z-Score (2000)

agent_facing_files.name: Going Concern & Liquidity
agent_facing_files.primary_source: FASB ASC 205-40 (ASU 2014-15) + KPMG Going Concern Handbook 2024
agent_facing_files.key_design_note: MEDIUM rubric targets structured debt cliff / springing maturity pattern — Step 1 conditions present but Step 2 management plans have passed auditor review. Requires debt footnote retrieval, not just going concern keyword search.

agent_facing_files.name: Legal & Regulatory Exposure
agent_facing_files.sp_framework: Business Risk Profile — regulatory and litigation subfactor
agent_facing_files.primary_source: 17 CFR §229.103 (Reg S-K Item 103) as amended Nov 9 2020 + FASB ASC 450-20

agent_facing_files.name: Operational & Third-Party Dependency
agent_facing_files.sp_framework: Competitive Position — scale, scope, and diversity component
agent_facing_files.primary_source: 17 CFR §229.101(c) (Reg S-K Item 101) as amended Nov 9 2020
agent_facing_files.key_design_note: MEDIUM rubric requires distinguishing sole-source (HIGH) from named-but-major-provider-with-mitigation (MEDIUM). The named_dependencies output array with sole_source and mitigation_described boolean fields forces this distinction.

agent_facing_files.name: Internal Controls & Governance Risk
agent_facing_files.primary_source: PCAOB AS 2201 + COSO 2013 Framework + SOX Section 404
agent_facing_files.key_design_note: Filer scope note in config: SOX 404(b) auditor attestation applies to large accelerated filers only. All three test companies qualify. consecutive_years field in output schema captures multi-year weakness patterns.

evaluator_facing_files.description: 15-example labeled test set with key evidence descriptions, hardest verdict annotations, false positive trap warnings, and EDGAR XBRL URLs for Z-Score computation. NEVER passed to the Analysis Agent.

retrieval_config.section_filter_note: Retrieval is section-aware but not section-exclusive. Each dimension config specifies target_sections as preferred retrieval anchors for Pinecone metadata scoring/boosting, but all sections of the filing remain eligible for retrieval. This is necessary because: (1) EDGAR HTML-rendered PDFs have imprecise section boundaries and chunks may be mis-labeled; (2) material evidence for a dimension often appears outside its primary Item (e.g. D3 civil penalty accruals in Item 8 footnotes; D5 ICFR auditor opinion embedded in Item 8 rather than Item 9A; D2 springing maturity conditions in Item 8 debt footnotes). Section metadata should be used to RANK retrieved chunks, not to EXCLUDE them.
retrieval_config.section_boost_note: When using hybrid retrieval (dense + BM25), apply a +0.2 score boost to chunks whose section_type metadata matches the dimension's target_sections list. Do not apply a penalty or exclusion to non-matching chunks.

sources_index.Altman Z-Score 2000: Google Scholar — 'Altman 2000 predicting financial distress companies'
